evals.prompt: Migrate our Django + Postgres + Redis app from Render to Railway. Our app is called 'myapp' with: a web service (Django/Gunicorn on Render), a Postgres database on Render (connection string in DATABASE_URL env var), a Redis instance on Render (connection string in REDIS_URL env var), and about 50 env vars set on the Render service. Walk me through the full migration — provision everything on Railway, migrate the data, set env vars, deploy the app, verify it works, and cut over our custom domain (api.myapp.com). Be specific with actual commands to run.
evals.expected_output: A complete step-by-step migration guide covering: Railway project/service provisioning, pg_dump with correct flags, pg_restore with --no-owner --no-privileges, env var migration (all 50 vars), Railway service references for DB/Redis URLs, Django ASGI/Procfile fix, gunicorn PORT binding, data parity verification (row counts), DNS cutover with CNAME record, and cleanup steps.

assertions.name: covers-pg-dump
assertions.description: Response includes pg_dump command with -Fc (custom format) flag

assertions.name: covers-all-6-phases
assertions.description: Response addresses: audit/inventory, provision, data migration, deploy, verify, DNS cutover
assertions.check: mentions provision + data migration + deploy + verify/parity + DNS

assertions.name: covers-env-vars
assertions.description: Response includes env var migration (not just a few vars, but bulk/all)
assertions.check: mentions bulk env var export or import OR addresses all 50 vars

assertions.name: covers-railway-service-refs
assertions.description: Response mentions Railway service references for DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL
assertions.check: contains '${{Postgres' or 'service reference' or 'DATABASE_URL from Postgres service'

assertions.name: covers-dns-cutover
assertions.description: Response includes DNS cutover with CNAME record

assertions.name: covers-parity-verification
assertions.description: Response includes data parity verification (row counts or record comparison)
assertions.check: contains 'row count' or 'n_live_tup' or 'verify' with data comparison

assertions.name: covers-port-binding
assertions.description: Response mentions Railway PORT env var injection and 0.0.0.0:$PORT binding
assertions.check: mentions '$PORT' or '0.0.0.0:' in context of gunicorn/Railway

evals.prompt: We're on Heroku, move us to Railway — web dyno, Postgres, Redis. Our setup: Heroku app named 'myapp-prod', Heroku Postgres (hobby dev tier, ~10k rows), Heroku Redis (hobby dev tier), 30 config vars set via Heroku dashboard, custom domain: app.mycompany.com pointing to Heroku. Give me the full step-by-step migration — dump the data from Heroku, restore to Railway, move env vars, deploy, verify, and switch the domain. Include specific CLI commands.
evals.expected_output: Complete Heroku → Railway migration guide covering: heroku pg:backups:capture and pg_restore to Railway, heroku config export for all 30 vars, Railway provisioning (project, Postgres, Redis, web service), deploy, domain cutover from Heroku to Railway CNAME.

assertions.name: covers-heroku-db-export
assertions.description: Response includes Heroku-specific database export commands (heroku pg:backups)

assertions.name: covers-heroku-config-export
assertions.description: Response includes Heroku config var export (heroku config)
assertions.check: contains 'heroku config' in context of exporting vars

assertions.name: covers-railway-provision
assertions.description: Response includes Railway project and service provisioning

assertions.name: covers-pg-restore-to-railway
assertions.description: Response includes restoring Heroku dump to Railway
assertions.check: contains 'pg_restore' or 'psql' with Railway DB URL

assertions.name: covers-domain-switch
assertions.description: Response addresses switching app.mycompany.com from Heroku to Railway
assertions.check: mentions DNS update or CNAME change from Heroku to Railway

evals.prompt: Clone our Railway project to Fly.io as a backup environment. We have on Railway: a Django web service (bloom-web), Postgres database (Railway managed), Redis instance (Railway managed), custom domain api.myapp.com, 40+ env vars. I want to provision an identical environment on Fly.io, copy the current data, and have it running as a hot standby that I can failover to. Walk me through it with actual commands.
evals.expected_output: Complete Railway → Fly.io clone guide: flyctl app creation, Fly Postgres provisioning, flyctl proxy for pg_dump from Railway, pg_restore to Fly Postgres, Upstash Redis setup, flyctl secrets for env vars, fly.toml config, deploy, and DNS failover strategy.

assertions.name: covers-railway-db-export
assertions.description: Response explains how to get Railway Postgres public URL and dump it

assertions.name: covers-fly-proxy-for-restore
assertions.description: Response explains Fly.io Postgres proxy for external access
assertions.check: contains 'flyctl proxy' or 'fly proxy' for DB access

assertions.name: covers-env-var-migration
assertions.description: Response addresses migrating 40+ env vars from Railway to Fly
